title: Over Railing DIY Planter Boxes
summary: These DIY planter boxes are the perfect entry level project and can be made with very basic materials from the home center!
description: These DIY planter boxes are the perfect entry level project and can be made with very basic materials from the home center!

materials.name: Cedar Dog-Ear Fence Picket, 19/32 in. D x 5-1/2 in. W x 6 ft. H

steps.title: Cut Parts
steps.body:
Using the cut list provided, cut down all of the pieces needs for the planters

**Tip:** Note Please note that these plans are outlined to make TWO planter boxes

steps.title: Attach Bottom Supports
steps.body: Using brad nails or screws, attach the bottom supports to one of the exterior pieces

steps.title: Attach Sides
steps.body: Using brad nails or pocket hole screws, attach the sides to the bottom support and exterior piece

steps.title: Attach 1 Exterior Side
steps.body: Using brad nails or pocket hole screws, attach one exterior side to the bottom support and bottom exterior piece

steps.title: Attach Side Supports
steps.body: Using brad nails or pocket hole screws, attach the sides to the side supports to the exterior pieces

steps.title: Attach Other Side
steps.body: Using brad nails or pocket hole screws, attach the last exterior piece to close in the side

steps.title: Attach Landscape Fabric
steps.body: Using staples, attach the landscape fabric to prevent all of the soil from being washed away during watering and rain

steps.title: Attach Hardware
steps.body: Drill out holes in the bottom of the planter box to accept the hardware to attach to the railing

steps.title: Attach to Railing
steps.body: Using the hardware from the previous step, attach the planter boxes over the railing

steps.title: Start Gardening!
steps.body: Your planter boxes are now done and ready for you to plant anything you want!
